content: Hanyu Shi is a PhD student of remote sensing at the Beijing Normal University. His research interests include radiative transfer and parameter estimation.
summary: Hanyu Shi is a PhD student of remote sensing at the Beijing Normal University. His research interests include radiative transfer and parameter estimation.
title: Hanyu Shi

content:
Current inversion methods are mostly designed to obtain a single parameter from remote-sensing data. Therefore, different parameters are retrieved using various models and algorithms that usually make different physical assumptions. These parameter-specific methods ignore physical connections among parameters, and therefore parameter values may lack physical consistency even though they were retrieved from the same sensor’s data.
Estimating multiple parameters using the same physical model has the potential to ensure physical consistency. A two-step estimation scheme is designed to estimate multiple parameter simultaneously.
summary: Current inversion methods are mostly designed to obtain a single parameter from remote-sensing data. Therefore, different parameters are retrieved using various models and algorithms that usually make different physical assumptions. These parameter-specific methods ignore physical connections among parameters, and therefore parameter values may lack physical consistency even though they were retrieved from the same sensor’s data.
title: Overview
